$schema: CaseLoad Select Core Chassis · Custom Values v1
description: Firm-level tokens used in every email subject, SMS body, and brief delivery. These resolve per-sub-account at runtime. Never hardcode firm data in templates; reference these tokens.

values.description: Full legal entity name. Used in compliance footers and engagement letters.
Short brand name for headers, subject lines, and casual references.
Primary firm phone in E.164 format.

values.example: 2323 Yonge Street, Suite 200, Toronto, ON M4P 2C9

values.description: Lawyer first name for personalized openings.
Display title for signatures and bios.
Direct cell for J1 Band A SMS delivery.
Direct lawyer email for sensitive communications.
Public consult booking link surfaced in J2 and Take.
Lawyer triage portal for Take/Pass decisions.
Client portal for retainer status and document access.

values.example: This communication does not create a solicitor-client relationship. No advice is provided until a written engagement letter is signed.
values.description: Footer text for every email per LSO Rule 4.2-1.

implementation_notes: Create each as a Custom Value in GHL Settings → Custom Values.
GHL renders these as {{custom_values.firm.legal_name}} in templates. The double-brace token name shown above is the canonical reference; GHL's UI will namespace under custom_values.
Example values are DRG Law placeholders for the first sub-account. CaseLoad Select master snapshot ships with empty values; each sub-account fills in its own at onboarding.
When a value contains URL or email content, validate format on import. GHL accepts longtext for disclaimer.
